rationale.R1.1: A general opinion on the research artifact centrally invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the proposed framework is conceptually coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation calls for judging whether the mechanism, ablations, and conclusions follow logically from the described setup.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact makes quantitative performance and ablation claims, evaluating whether those reported results support the stated conclusions is central.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion should preserve uncertainty about missing experimental, methodological, and prior-work details.
rationale.RX.2: The response responsibility includes avoiding endorsement of broad claims such as generalization across language pairs beyond what the artifact establishes.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within giving an opinion on the research artifact rather than executing edits or expanding the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Assessment should avoid encouraging unsupported deployment or misleading claims about translation quality and generalization.
rationale.RX.5: Judgments should remain traceable to claims visible in the provided artifact rather than unstated assumptions.
